source.title: Attack video on Border — Bathroom/Tellers site via top-floor hatch drop
source.notes: Single-site walkthrough covering the Bathroom/Tellers site (same site as border_defense.json's 'bathroom-tellers'), attacked entirely from the top floor by taking Armory/Archives/Office, dropping the hatch above the bathroom shower stall, and planting from above. The creator plays 'Book' in the in-game footage purely for map-manipulation convenience, not as a strategy pick — Book is not listed as an operator recommendation below. Auto-generated captions: 'Candala'/'candela' = Ying's Candela; 'Kore'/'Kclaw'/'Cade claw' = Kaid's K-EMP hard-electrified hatch/claw (Kaid himself is referenced only as a defender the attackers may ban or need EMP against, never as an attack pick); 'hive canister' = Grim's Hard Breach Grenade / Kawan Hive launcher call-out; 'air jab'/'air jabs' = Nomad's Airjab Launcher. Ground-floor rooms (Ventilation/Workshop, Customs/Supply) are explicitly described by the creator as irrelevant to this attack and are not detailed here. No reliable top-down blueprint grounding was available for this map during the rewrite pass, so location strings are room/feature names only — no compass directions have been added.

hatches.location: Hatch above the Bathroom Shower Stall
hatches.note: Primary objective — drop this hatch to plant in the shower stall below. Ignore everything else on the ground floor.

gadgetPlacements.note: Any of the three carries the hard-breach charge to open the hatch — interchangeable. If electrified, bring EMPs on Grim (or ban Kaid pre-round) since a thrown Thatcher charge won't reliably detonate it here.

gadgetPlacements.location: Floor near the Hatch, Angled Throw
gadgetPlacements.note: Cluster Charges thrown at an angle bounce underneath the hatch and generally destroy a Kaid K-EMP claw if present. Not 100% reliable — depends on bounce direction.
Drone defenders out of half wall. Use a Grim canister or Ying Candela to flush anyone holding there once spotted.
Make a higher hole for a line on half wall instead of only opening the door.
If a defender retreats from half wall into Small Office, use this soft wall to keep pressure on and cut them off.
Drone in and clear that side while Ying covers the other flank. Can also be human-droned by a fifth player on Monty.
If defenders fall back into back Archives, make a soft-wall hole here as another cutoff toward Office.
Fully soft — manipulate as needed to gain final control of Office.

Drop and continuously replace a drone here for the entire setup phase — watches both the top of Metal Stairs and the top of East Stairs. Mandatory; replace immediately if shot. A Zero cam can substitute for one of Grim/Ying/Monty in this role.
Airjab Launcher to deny the rotate. Only triggers on line of sight — can be shot or avoided, the 90 drone backs it up.
Second Airjab Launcher covering the other stairwell flank, same reliability caveats.

gadgetPlacements.location: Wall Overlooking the Bathroom Door/Corridor, near the Plant
gadgetPlacements.note: Cluster Charges thrown through this wall send pellets through the bathroom door and destroy any utility/shields set up before the plant goes down.
Make this rotate hole after the hatch is open for a line of sight onto the plant area/feet of anyone pushing the workshop rotate. Go prone to see feet even on angles that look blocked.
Shoot a second line of sight here to cover the site door rotate. Shotgun makes carving two lines of sight faster.

operatorRecommendations.note: Interchangeable with Ying and Fuse for opening the hatch. Hive canister flushes defenders from half wall and other holds. Can be dropped if only running a 3-stack.
Candela flushes contested rooms (half wall, Small Office). Also carries hard-breach charges as an alternative to Grim/Fuse for the hatch.
Opens the hatch and, angled correctly, clears a Kaid K-EMP claw via cluster charge bounce. Also fuses the bathroom-door wall pre-plant to destroy defender utility.
Places the 90 Hallway drone (must be watched and replaced continuously) and both Airjab Launchers on Top Metal and Top East Stairs to lock down both rotate flanks.
Walks through the Archives door as a human drone to clear Armory or push into Office. Enter through the doorway, not a window — a shield is vulnerable jumping through windows.
Can substitute for Grim, Ying, or Monty in a 5-stack. Places a camera to help hold the 90 Hallway watch duty.

generalTips.note: Minimum 3 operators needed (drop Grim or Ying if only 3). Ideal is 4 (Grim, Ying, Fuse, Nomad), a 5th (Monty or Zero) makes the roam clear easier. With 3, you can only actively watch 2 of the 3 post-plant angles.
Only Armory, Archives, and Office need to be taken/cleared. Balcony, Break Room, CCTV, and most of 90 Hallway can be ignored — a single 90 drone denies both flanking stairs.
The only angle worth caring about from the ignored side of the map is a sightline from Balcony into the Office double door.
Two players push Armory (Fuse + Grim/Ying), two push Archives/Office (Nomad + Ying/Grim). Drone each other in, flush contested rooms with Grim canisters/Ying Candela, use windows/doors as cutoffs.
Once Armory, Archives, Fountain, and Office are cleared and the 90 drone is set up, the hatch execute is close to guaranteed — creator claims roughly 80-85% win rate on this site.

After dropping and planting, one attacker holds the plant while the other three hold three angles: the workshop rotate, the sight door, and the drone hole itself (go prone to reveal defenders' feet through the drone hole). With only 3 total, cover the rotate and sight door and listen for footsteps on the third.
Almost every role is interchangeable outside the hard-breach-and-declaw duty — contrasted against the Bank Thermite/Osa attack where losing Thermite breaks the whole plan.

Once the plant is down with three attackers still alive, watch the diffuser, keep someone on the 90 drone/cams to prevent a retake pushing up Metal or East, and don't hesitate to throw remaining Grim/Ying utility to protect the plant.

Defender counterplay to anticipate: playing from Talis behind feet holes to deny both corner plant spots in Tellers; a smoke thrown precisely through the drone hole shuts down the main plant spot; C4 through the drone hole is less reliable since the hole absorbs part of the blast.
